directive_processed.title: PROP-022 phase 5 + phase 6 detail amendment — resolve internal inconsistency, supply missing code-change specs, define regression tests

context_gathering: Read PROP-022 (707 lines, 30K tokens) — full proposal absorbed.
Read state-file-archives.md (108 lines) — current convention status verified.
Audited expansion-tracker.json (290 items, 9 status values) — confirmed multi-writer + canonical next_id field + integrated:true discriminator.
Audited closed-issues.json (1518 issues, 1.4MB) — confirmed ID format `"id": "ISS-NNN"` with 4 lookup-by-id consumers.
Audited verify-pending-state.js (291 lines) — confirmed SCAN_TARGETS pattern + flip-only mutation today.
Traced 5 closed-issues consumer call sites in analyst.md, curmudgeon.md, decider-intake.md, decider-patches-and-selfapply.md, social.md.
Traced 3 expansion-tracker consumer call sites in analyst.md, decider.md / decider-curmudgeon.md, structure-integrity.md (5 sub-checks in §7).
Pulled commit messages for 61b23c0, b13d162, bdc733b via GitHub API — confirmed phases 1-3 used single-commit pattern (no dual-write).
Benchmarked grep-on-JSONL: ≤2-3ms on 1.4MB and 5MB archives (10-20× faster than current JSON.parse-and-find at 20-40ms).

phase_5_gaps: gap_1: complete-vs-integrated inconsistency — RESOLVED with integrated:true discriminator
gap_2: structure-integrity §7 two-pass code — RESOLVED with explicit before/after for 5 sub-checks
gap_3: discipline 1a verifier code — RESOLVED with verify-pending-state.js v1.1.0 spec (Option A: archive-aware verifier)
gap_4: EXP-NNN ID generation — RESOLVED (next_id stays in live, with archive-aware self-heal)
gap_5: regression test plan — DEFINED (5 tests with pre/post capture commands)
phase_6_gaps: gap_1: stale-prerequisite reconciliation — RESOLVED (phases 1-5 + 7 days post-phase-5)
gap_2: grep-pattern correctness — VERIFIED across 5 call sites with one caveat (event-count vs unique-count)
gap_3: wall-clock acceptance threshold — DEFINED with empirical benchmarks (≤10ms single, ≤15ms bulk-5)
gap_4: dual-write window — ARGUMENT GIVEN (skip if phase 5 stable for 7+ days)

findings.description: PROP-022-amendment-001 authored and committed-ready. 5 phase-5 gaps + 4 phase-6 gaps resolved with explicit before/after code blocks. Awaiting operator review.
findings.recommended_action: Operator reviews amendment. On approval, schedule phase 5 implementation per the implementation_order_recommendation section (phase_5_a backfill + state-file-archives.md table, phase_5_b code changes; wait 7 days; phase_6_a + phase_6_b).

findings.description: While auditing for the amendment, surfaced a subtle correctness issue in PROP-022's pre-push integrity gate (decider-patches-and-selfapply.md Invariant 1): post-phase-5, max-id check on live alone could mask a missing-next_id bug if live array were nearly empty after a wave of integrations. Amendment specifies the archive-aware fix.
findings.recommended_action: Operator approves the gate fix as part of phase 5 implementation.

findings.description: verify-pending-state.js needs both a writer-side change (archive-append on flip) AND a reader-side change (post-phase-6, walk JSONL archive's last-event-per-id for /-pending-verification$/ status). Phase 6's verifier change is the largest single code-change in the migration. Amendment specifies the SCAN_TARGETS 4-tuple extension and v1.1.0 (phase 5) → v1.2.0 (phase 6) version path.
findings.recommended_action: Operator approves the verifier change as part of phase 6 implementation. Treat it as the highest-risk single change in phase 6 — exhaustively unit-test before commit.

previous_followup._note: Per tinker.md, walk monitor/tinker/proposals/ and run each PROP's verification_pattern. Skipped this run because directive-driven Mode 4 work consumed the full window. Next BAU Mode 1 run should pick up PROP lifecycle verification.

next_run_priority: BAU Mode 1 (pipeline health) or Mode 3 (cost engineering) per operator schedule. If operator approves the amendment quickly, next directive run will likely focus on phase 5 implementation (assuming a phase-5-implementation directive issues).
